name: Guan Yu
description: Guan Yu, also known as Guan Gong or Guan Yunchang, is a revered historical figure in Chinese culture, elevated to the status of a deity in Chinese folk religion and Taoism. Born during the late Eastern Han dynasty, Guan Yu served as a general under the warlord Liu Bei. He is famed for his unwavering loyalty, righteousness, and martial prowess. Guan Yu's legendary feats include slaying Yan Liang during the Battle of Boma, and his escape from Cao Cao's forces after being captured. He is often depicted with a long beard, a red face, and wielding a guandao, a polearm also known as the Green Dragon Crescent Blade. His commitment to his sworn oath with Liu Bei and Zhang Fei in the Peach Garden has become a symbol of brotherhood and fidelity. After his death, Guan Yu's spirit became widely venerated, believed to possess protective powers and the ability to bring wealth and prosperity. Temples dedicated to Guan Yu are found throughout China and in Chinese communities worldwide, where he is worshipped as a god of war, wealth, and loyalty. His story is prominently featured in the historical novel Romance of the Three Kingdoms, further cementing his legendary status and influencing generations of Chinese culture. He represents integrity, courage, and unyielding commitment to justice, making him a powerful and enduring symbol in Chinese society.
subtitle: Loyalty, Righteousness, and Martial Prowess

keyMyths.title: Peach Garden Oath
keyMyths.description: Guan Yu, Liu Bei, and Zhang Fei swore an oath of brotherhood in a peach garden, pledging loyalty and mutual support until death. This oath became a cornerstone of their relationship and a symbol of unwavering brotherhood.

keyMyths.title: Slaying Yan Liang
keyMyths.description: During the Battle of Boma, Guan Yu single-handedly charged into the enemy ranks and slew the formidable general Yan Liang. This act demonstrated his exceptional martial prowess and bravery.

keyMyths.title: Guan Yu's Captivity
keyMyths.description: After being captured by Cao Cao, Guan Yu refused to betray his oath to Liu Bei and eventually escaped to rejoin his sworn brother. This event highlights his unwavering loyalty and integrity.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.description: A historical novel that greatly popularized Guan Yu's story and cemented his legendary status.
sources.text: A historical novel that greatly popularized Guan Yu's story and cemented his legendary status.
